pending.body: finding_summary: Mixed short/qualified bead IDs are only normalized on the read side. `record_task_run_start` can discover a prior finalized attempt via canonicalized matching, but `reopen_failed_exact_attempt` still filters raw rows with `entry.bead_id == bead_id` at src/adapters/fs.rs:4541-4550, and `finalize_task_run_internal` does the same at src/adapters/fs.rs:4693-4699. If lineage stored `bead-1` and the current task uses `ms-alpha.bead-1` (or vice versa), retries and completion/repair syncs fail with "already finalized" or "no matching task run" even though the attempt was found. severity: high. affected_files: [src/adapters/fs.rs]. remediation_hint: Canonicalize bead refs in the raw-row reopen/finalize paths too, and add retry/complete tests that mix short and qualified bead IDs.

finding_summary: Snapshot reconciliation still treats short and qualified bead refs as different beads. `latest_task_runs_per_bead` keys by raw `entry.bead_id` at src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:554-568, and `reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage` uses that output for `active_bead` and progress counts at src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:953-989. A retry history containing both `bead-1` and `ms-alpha.bead-1` for one logical bead will be double-counted, and two active mixed-form rows can even trip the "multiple active beads present in lineage" corruption path. severity: medium. affected_files: [src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs, src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs]. remediation_hint: Group latest runs by canonical milestone bead ref rather than the raw stored string.

finding_summary: `project show` / `task show` now fail outright when a bead-linked project's milestone record is missing. `show_project` unconditionally loads lineage at src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1363-1417, and `read_bead_lineage` returns `MilestoneNotFound` as soon as the milestone directory is absent at src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2222-2237. That turns an otherwise readable project into an error even though the project record, run snapshot, and raw milestone/bead IDs still exist. severity: medium. affected_files: [src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs, src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs]. remediation_hint: Degrade to `task_lineage: None` or a partial lineage stub when the milestone record is missing instead of aborting the whole detail query.

show_project (service.rs:1412-1417) propagates MilestoneNotFound as a hard error via `?` on load_task_lineage_detail. This means `project show` and `task show` become completely unusable for any task whose referenced milestone has been deleted or whose milestone_id is invalid. The test `show_project_propagates_missing_milestone_lineage_errors` confirms this is intentional, but it breaks the backward-compat guarantee: previously `show_project` succeeded for all existing projects regardless of milestone state. A project with task_source referencing a stale milestone should still be inspectable — the lineage should fall back to None (like when the plan is corrupt/missing) rather than failing the entire operation. Fix: in `load_task_lineage_detail`, catch `MilestoneNotFound` errors and return `Ok(None)` instead of propagating, matching the graceful fallback pattern already used for corrupt/missing plans.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 6 -> Plan and Implement
